layman_headline: Market fear lingers — no high-conviction swing trade setup right now, staying patient.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in a fear phase with Bitcoin dominance at 56.5%, which means money is flowing away from altcoins and into Bitcoin. Most of the swing trade candidates are either in downtrends with bearish chart structures, or have already rallied sharply and look extended. The best potential setup is Ethereum near oversold RSI levels, but it's still below all its key moving averages and just bounced 7% — making a chase entry risky. We're better off waiting for a pullback to stronger support.
layman_action: Stay on the sidelines with cash ready. If Ethereum pulls back to the $1,800 support level, that becomes a much better entry point with a clear stop-loss below $1,720. Don't chase coins that have already bounced 20-40% this week.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a compelling risk/reward right now. LDO and 币安人生 have the best chart bias scores (+0.85 and +0.80), but both have already extended significantly (LDO +51.7% in 5 days, 币安人生 has a poor 0.38x and 0.97x risk/reward ratio respectively). PUMP has a Morning Star pattern but is up 40% in 7 days — chasing that is dangerous. ETH's RSI at 39 is our most reliable signal type (80% win rate historically), but the chart bias is -0.70 with price below all MAs and resistance at $1,900 right overhead. The smart play is to wait for ETH to retest $1,800 support, which would push RSI closer to genuinely oversold territory and provide a much better risk/reward entry. BTC dominance at 56.5% continues to suppress alt performance broadly.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.5% is actively suppressing altcoin performance — any BTC correction could drag the entire market down
Bitcoin hitting $65K resistance with institutional tech sell-off in equities could trigger a broader risk-off move
Fear & Greed at 29 is improving but still in fear territory — sentiment could easily reverse if BTC rejects at resistance
watch_list: ETH price action at $1,800 support — a bounce there with rising volume would be an ideal entry
BTC behavior at $65,000 resistance — a breakout lifts everything, a rejection could create better buying opportunities
Fear & Greed index trajectory — if it crosses above 35, it signals sentiment shift that supports risk-on altcoin trades
market_context: Bitcoin is testing $65K resistance while equities face institutional selling pressure. The market is in fear (29/100) but improving, with BTC dominance at 56.5% keeping altcoins suppressed — not ideal conditions for aggressive alt entries.
context_banner: High BTC dominance and fearful sentiment make this a patience cycle — the best trades come from waiting for clean support bounces, not chasing extended moves.

key_signals.description: ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal historically (80% win rate) — but needs to reach support at $1,800 for a proper setup.
Fear & Greed at 29 (Fear) with improving trend from 25.9 weekly average suggests sentiment is bottoming but hasn't turned yet.
ETH derivatives reset article signals cleaner positioning in Ethereum markets, which could reduce liquidation risk on the next move up.
Bitcoin hitting $65K wall amid record institutional tech sell-off creates macro uncertainty that warrants caution before new entries.
Best chart scores belong to LDO (+0.85) and 币安人生 (+0.80), but both have poor risk/reward ratios (0.38x and 0.97x) — not suitable for swing entries.
